raw_json: {"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_preference","object_kind":"financial_strategy","confidence":0.84,"matched_text":"Using your cashback earnings to offset household expenses or save up for future purchases is a smart and practical way to make the most of your rewards.","distilled_fact":"The speaker prefers using cashback earnings to offset household expenses or save up for future purchases."},{"signal_kind":"potential_preference","object_kind":"financial_belief","confidence":0.77,"matched_text":"It's also a great way to think of cashback earnings as a way to \"earn\" money back on your everyday purchases, rather than just seeing it as a one-time discount.","distilled_fact":"The speaker thinks cashback earnings should be viewed as money earned back on everyday purchases rather than as a one-time discount."},{"signal_kind":"potential_preference","object_kind":"budgeting_style","confidence":0.8,"matched_text":"having a little extra cash on hand can be a huge stress-reliever.","distilled_fact":"The speaker values having a little extra cash on hand."},{"signal_kind":"potential_preference","object_kind":"budgeting_style","confidence":0.73,"matched_text":"it's always nice to have some wiggle room in your budget for unexpected expenses or to take advantage of a great deal.","distilled_fact":"The speaker prefers having wiggle room in a budget for unexpected expenses or to take advantage of a great deal."}]}
